title: [supervisor] Implement live-session ownership and nudge routing
objective: Build/prove the routing rule from `.idea`: discover whether a failing PR is owned by a live/original session or existing lane; send that session an exact failure-payload nudge when ownership is clear; otherwise spawn or assign a clean repair worker lane.
acceptance_criteria: Ownership discovery checks current meta worktrees, branch ownership metadata, PR author/head ref, and any available live session registry before assignment.
A live-session nudge template is materialized with PR URL, failing checks, log links/excerpts, scope, validation commands, and stop conditions.
If ownership is stale/unclear, the supervisor records why a clean worker is required and creates/updates the repair lane instead of reviving stale context.
No PR can have more than one active repair owner unless supervisor explicitly reclassifies it as a shared incident.

source_gap_audit.reason: Expanded from .handoff/.idea intent after the initial TASK-PRR set proved too broad.
